UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the DocuExtract application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded document or image for text extraction and analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this document. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Document)

originalFileName.description: The original name of the file uploaded by the user.

storageUrl.description: URL or path where the original document file is stored.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

documentType.description: Categorization of the document type to guide AI processing. Can be 'identityProof', 'billing', 'medicalReport', or 'general'.

status.description: Current processing status of the document. Can be 'uploaded', 'processing', 'extracted', or 'error'.

extractedText.description: The full raw text extracted from the document by OCR and AI.

lastEditedAt.description: Timestamp of the last manual edit made to the extracted text.

IdentityProofExtraction.title: IdentityProofExtraction
IdentityProofExtraction.description: Stores extracted personal details from identity proof documents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IdentityProofExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document entity this extraction belongs to. (Relationship: Document 1:1 IdentityProofExtraction)

extractedAt.description: Timestamp when the identity proof details were extracted.

fullName.description: The full name extracted from the identity document.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth extracted from the identity document.

address.description: The address extracted from the identity document.

documentNumber.description: The unique identification number (e.g., passport number, driver's license number) from the document.

documentType.description: The specific type of identity document (e.g., 'Passport', 'Driver\'s License', 'National ID Card').

issueDate.description: The date the identity document was issued.

expiryDate.description: The date the identity document expires.

BillingExtraction.title: BillingExtraction
BillingExtraction.description: Stores extracted financial details from billing documents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BillingExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document entity this extraction belongs to. (Relationship: Document 1:1 BillingExtraction)

extractedAt.description: Timestamp when the billing details were extracted.

vendorName.description: The name of the vendor or service provider on the bill.

billDate.description: The date the bill was issued.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the bill.

currency.description: The currency of the total amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

billingAddress.description: The billing address provided on the document.

BillingLineItem.title: BillingLineItem
BillingLineItem.description: Represents a single item or service listed on a billing document.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BillingLineItem entity.

billingExtractionId.description: Reference to the BillingExtraction entity this line item belongs to. (Relationship: BillingExtraction 1:N BillingLineItem)

description.description: Description of the item or service.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item or service.

lineTotal.description: The total cost for this specific line item (quantity * unitPrice).

MedicalReportExtraction.title: MedicalReportExtraction
MedicalReportExtraction.description: Stores extracted medical information, diagnoses, and findings from hospital reports.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalReportExtraction entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document entity this extraction belongs to. (Relationship: Document 1:1 MedicalReportExtraction)

extractedAt.description: Timestamp when the medical report details were extracted.

patientName.description: The name of the patient mentioned in the report.

dateOfReport.description: The date the medical report was issued.

diagnosis.description: The primary diagnosis or condition identified in the report.

treatmentRecommendations.description: Recommended treatments or next steps based on the report.

doctorName.description: The name of the doctor or physician who issued the report.

hospitalName.description: The name of the hospital or clinic where the report originated.

keyFindings.description: A summary of key findings or observations from the medical report.

PageHit.title: PageHit
PageHit.description: Represents a hit count for a specific page.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`{userId}`) for each UserProfile document directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID, ensuring path-based ownership. This collection forms the root of a user's private data hierarchy.
Stores hit counts for application pages.

Contains documents uploaded by a specific user. This subcollection is nested under the respective UserProfile, establishing a clear ownership hierarchy. Authorization is achieved via the `{userId}` in the path. The `userId` field within the document serves as an explicit data link and redundancy.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded document.

definition.description: Stores extracted personal details from identity proof documents. This is a subcollection of a specific Document, implicitly inheriting its `userId` and `documentId` from the parent path for authorization independence. The `documentId` field within the extraction document provides an explicit data link.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the document and extraction.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent document.

params.name: extractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the identity proof extraction.

definition.description: Stores extracted financial details from billing documents. This is a subcollection of a specific Document, implicitly inheriting its `userId` and `documentId` from the parent path for authorization independence. The `documentId` field within the extraction document provides an explicit data link.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the document and extraction.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent document.

params.name: extractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the billing extraction.

definition.description: Stores individual line items associated with a specific BillingExtraction. Nested under its parent BillingExtraction, this structure ensures strong data consistency and inherits authorization from the upstream user and document IDs.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and extraction.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent document.

params.name: billingExtractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent billing extraction.

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the billing line item.

definition.description: Stores extracted medical information, diagnoses, and findings from hospital reports. This is a subcollection of a specific Document, implicitly inheriting its `userId` and `documentId` from the parent path for authorization independence. The `documentId` field within the extraction document provides an explicit data link.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the document and extraction.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent document.

params.name: extractionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the medical report extraction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling, prioritizing a secure, scalable, and debuggable system for DocuExtract. The primary strategy employed is **Path-Based Ownership** and deep hierarchical nesting for all user-specific data.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** Authorization independence is achieved primarily through the hierarchical structure. Every piece of user-specific data (documents, and their corresponding extractions) is nested directly under a user's unique ID (`/users/{userId}/...`). This means that the `userId` is always part of the document path. Consequently, Firestore Security Rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents to determine ownership or permissions. For instance, to check if a user can access a `Document` or an `IdentityProofExtraction`, the rule simply compares the authenticated user's UID with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, making rules atomic, faster, and easier to debug.

While the entity schemas include `userId` in `Document` and `documentId` in extraction entities, these serve primarily for data integrity, querying flexibility, and client-side data modeling rather than direct authorization in rules. The path itself carries the necessary authorization context.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure ensures that security rules are not used as filters. All documents for a specific user are stored under their unique path, `/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}`. When a user queries for their documents (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/documents`), the query itself is scoped to their data. The security rule (`allow read: if request.auth.uid == userId;`) simply enforces that only the owner can access their subcollection. There are no public documents mixed with private ones, which would necessitate filtering on document fields. This homogeneous security posture within each user's hierarchy allows for efficient and secure `list` operations, as the server-side rules will only grant access to data already partitioned by the user's ID.

**Structural Segregation:** All application data is fundamentally private and owned by individual users. There are no
